app.fastTooltip: Fast mode uses {effort} effort for new turns
app.reasoningDescription: Adjust model reasoning effort for the next turn.
app.title: Codex
auth.haveAccount: Already have an account? Sign in
auth.totpHint: Enter the 6-digit code from Google Authenticator.
chat.emptySubtitle: Start from a focused coding workflow, or type your own request in the composer below.
chat.prompt.build.detail: Plan, implement, and verify a focused change.
chat.prompt.build.label: Build feature
chat.prompt.build.prompt: Build a new feature in this project. Start by inspecting the relevant files, then implement and verify the change.
chat.prompt.explore.detail: Map the architecture and important files.
chat.prompt.explore.label: Explore code
chat.prompt.explore.prompt: Explore this repository and summarize the architecture, key entry points, and likely places to modify.
chat.prompt.fix.detail: Reproduce the issue and land a tested fix.
chat.prompt.fix.label: Fix failures
chat.prompt.fix.prompt: Find and fix the current failure. Reproduce it first, make the smallest safe change, and run the relevant tests.
chat.prompt.review.detail: Find risks, regressions, and missing tests.
chat.prompt.review.label: Review changes
chat.requestsEmpty: No requests yet. New turns will appear here with last and total tokens.
chat.requestsSubtitle: Live turn status and token usage
chat.working.commandDetail: Codex is waiting for command output
chat.working.headline: Working ({elapsed} • esc to interrupt)
chat.working.thinkingDetail: Codex is preparing its next step
chat.working.toolDetail: Codex is waiting for tool output
composer.attachTooltip: Attach files to this turn. Images, PDF, Office documents, and text files are supported.
composer.fullAutoNotice: Full Auto is active for this conversation. No sandbox boundary and no approval prompts will be used for Codex actions.
composer.placeholder: Ask Codex to inspect, edit, test, or explain this workspace...
composer.setupDescription: These settings are applied when a new Codex thread is created. Existing threads keep their original setup.
composer.shortcutsDescription: Enter sends, or appends while Codex is running. Shift+Enter inserts a new line. Esc stops the running turn. Arrow Up/Down recalls composer history.
engine.notReady: Codex engine is not ready yet. You can type now; sending will unlock when it is ready.
history.costNoUsage: No token usage reported for this conversation yet.
history.emptyDescription: Codex history will appear here after threads are created.
history.emptySearch: No history rows match this search.
history.engineEmptyDescription: Run that CLI (or its *-launch wrapper) once, then refresh.
history.engineNeedToken: Sign in to load other CLI histories on this host.
history.transcriptEmpty: No messages parsed from this session store.
history.tuiEmptyDescription: Run Codex in the terminal once, then refresh.
newChat.label: New Chat
onboarding.codex.auth.detail: After the first successful reply, configure members, relay access, balances, and permissions (skip if you are the only user).
onboarding.codex.auth.title: Set authorization if needed
onboarding.codex.official.detail: Sign in with an official OpenAI key for direct Responses API access.
onboarding.codex.official.title: Official OpenAI
onboarding.codex.official.verifySuccess: Official OpenAI login saved and activated.
onboarding.codex.relay.detail: Enter Base URL, API Key, models, and rate multipliers. This is the first beginner step before chatting.
onboarding.codex.relay.title: Configure the relay
onboarding.codex.subtitle: Three steps: configure a relay → send one test message and wait for a reply → set authorization if needed.
onboarding.codex.test.detail: Use a tiny prompt to verify session, relay, model mapping, and streaming — then wait for the first reply.
onboarding.codex.test.prompt: Say hello once and reply with the active model name, relay provider, and whether streaming works.
onboarding.codex.test.title: Send one test turn
onboarding.codex.title: Quick start for beginners
settings.appearance.activeSkinDescriptio: Official themes are always installed. Local/customer skins can be installed and switched here.
settings.appearance.skinSafetyDescriptio: Theme plugins and API relay providers are independent. Restoring Light/Black never rewrites provider keys or base URLs.
settings.appearance.subtitle: Official skins plus installable and user-defined theme plugins
settings.assistant.chooseModelDescriptio: Choose a dedicated provider and model before enabling Setting Assistant actions.
settings.assistant.modelDescription: A separate model/provider for settings help, parsing screenshots, importing relay rates, theme creation, MCP/plugins, launch adapters, and other Settings domains.
settings.assistant.noProviderDescription: Log in to official OpenAI or add a relay provider before using Setting Assistant.
settings.assistant.readyDescription: Dedicated settings model: {provider} / {model}. Assistant actions will use this provider instead of the active chat model.
settings.assistant.title: Setting Assistant
settings.audit.subtitle: Recent high-risk thread/start and turn/start calls recorded locally
settings.audit.title: Dangerous permission audit
settings.codex.configCoverageDescription: Quick settings keep the common controls compact. All config is generated from Codex's bundled JSON schema and includes runtime keys from config/read.
settings.codex.configSourceDescription: Engine-owned keys only. Provider secrets use keyring; skins use local theme plugins.
settings.codex.editJsonHelp: Edit as JSON and blur to save.
settings.codex.engineReadyNotice: Open Settings while the engine is ready to load live user config.
settings.codex.memberReadOnly: Members can use Codex with the admin-managed configuration, but cannot edit config.toml settings.
settings.codex.quick.approvalPolicy.desc: Default approval_policy for new threads (string forms only).
settings.codex.quick.instructions.descri: Persisted instructions prepended for Codex sessions.
settings.codex.quick.instructions.label: User instructions
settings.codex.quick.model.description: Codex user config model default (provider activation may override for the active session).
settings.codex.quick.model.label: Default model
settings.codex.quick.modelProvider.descr: Engine model_provider key. Third-party relays are activated from the Config inspector, not rewritten as API keys here.
settings.codex.quick.modelProvider.label: Model provider
settings.codex.quick.reviewModel.descrip: Optional review_model used for review sessions.
settings.codex.quick.reviewModel.label: Review model
settings.codex.quick.sandboxMode.label: Sandbox mode
settings.codex.quick.serviceTier.label: Service tier
settings.codex.quick.webSearch.label: Web search
settings.codex.subtitle: Synced through config/read and config/batchWrite
settings.codex.userConfigSyncDescription: Curated fields write into the real Codex user config.toml with reloadUserConfig. Theme skins and API keys never enter this path.
settings.launch.adminOnlyMutate: Detection is available. Installing or writing .env requires an admin account.
settings.launch.afterInstallHint: After install: edit ~/.config/code-launch/.env (CODE_LAUNCH_BASE_URL / MODEL / API_KEY), ensure ~/.local/bin is on PATH, then run code-launch for CLI or keep using this UI after configuring the local proxy base URL.
settings.launch.catalogInfo: These are not Codex marketplace plugins. They are open-source local launchers/proxies. For Codex React UI, start with code-launch; other *-launch tools target other CLIs.
settings.launch.catalogSubtitle: Download helpers from {org}. Pattern: {pattern}. code-launch is required for this UI when relays are Chat Completions only.
settings.launch.downloadSectionHint: Each adapter: https://github.com/layola13/xxxxx-launch — clone, ./install.sh, then configure .env.
settings.launch.engineActiveNote: Active engine: {engine}. Chat uses Codex Responses (pair with code-launch when relays are Chat Completions only).
settings.launch.engineHint: Switch the main chat engine. Only Codex is available now; agy / auggie / claude / crush / grok / gemini (Chat protocol) are planned.
settings.launch.enginePlannedNote: {engine} is planned (protocol {protocol}, adapter {launch}). UI chat comes later — you can download the launcher below first.
settings.launch.engineRoadmap: Roadmap: default Codex → multi-engine chat (agy, auggie, claude, crush, grok, gemini) → optional arena mode. Same relay and membership model.
settings.launch.envModeHint: Shared: same BASE_URL / MODEL / API_KEY expanded to each adapter prefix. Separate: fill per adapter. None: only clone + install.sh.
settings.launch.includeAutomationHistory: By default, only terminal Cli and web VSCode sessions are listed. Enabling this includes automated Exec and AppServer threads (subagents and unknown sources are always excluded).
settings.launch.needToken: Sign in (or open with session token) to detect and install host launchers.
settings.launch.noPublicRepo: GitHub repo not published under layola13 — cannot one-click clone. Install from a local path or publish the repo first.
settings.launch.oneClickHint: Pending install/config: {missing}; selected now: {selected}. Source clones go to {root}. Flow: git clone → ./install.sh → write ~/.config/<name>/.env
settings.launch.pageSubtitle: Install from {org} (pattern {pattern}). Multi-engine chat is planned; this UI is Codex-only today.
settings.launch.relayBannerBody: Codex React UI speaks the Responses API. Many third-party relays only expose Chat Completions. Install code-launch from {repo} (family: {pattern}) so traffic is translated, then point the launcher at this relay's base URL / model / API key.
settings.launch.selectForSeparateEnv: Select adapters above to fill separate .env fields.
settings.launch.skipCli: Skip product CLI install if already installed (--skip-cli)
settings.launch.subtitle: Download layola13/*-launch bridges. Engine switching roadmap: Codex only for now.
settings.layout.assistantUsageDetailsDes: Show the detailed token and billing chip row under each answer. Off by default because the answer header already shows the summary.
settings.layout.historyPanelDescription: Show or hide the left conversation panel. Width is draggable on desktop.
settings.layout.rightWorkspaceDescriptio: Side chat, Browser, and Terminal stay outside Settings and open only from the top-right split-view control.
settings.layout.subtitle: Main workbench layout preferences; the right runtime workspace opens from the toolbar
settings.layout.workspaceCwdDescription: Used for new threads, file tools, skills and permission roots.
settings.members.securityNote: Safe default: app-layer caps + per-member workspace under ~/.codex-react-ui/members. Do not auto-create host Linux SSH/sudo accounts for members.
settings.members.subtitle: Admin-only member accounts, permission caps, and isolation roots.
settings.members.yourCapsDescription: Server enforces these limits on every thread/start and turn/start.
settings.permissions.adminAllRelays: Admins can use every relay; no whitelist needed.
settings.permissions.allowedRelaysHint: Only checked relays can be listed and activated by this member. Empty means no relay access.
settings.permissions.empty: No members match the current filters.
settings.permissions.noProviders: No relays configured yet. Add relays in Relay settings first.
settings.permissions.passwordOptional: Leave blank to keep current password
settings.permissions.subtitle: Create members, set capabilities/credits/concurrency, and choose which relays each member may use.
settings.permissions.title: Member system
settings.pet.subtitle: Optional companion surface independent of chat layout
settings.pet.threeDockDescription: A small independent scene can be enabled for a companion/status surface.
settings.plugins.composerMentionsDescrip: Insert an installed and enabled Codex plugin mention into the main composer.
settings.plugins.hooksDescription: Hooks are resolved from Codex config and installed plugin contributions for the current workspace.
settings.plugins.mcpDescription: Servers are resolved from Codex config and enabled plugin selections.
settings.plugins.noApps: No plugin apps are currently exposed by installed Codex plugins.
settings.plugins.noHooks: No Codex hooks are currently returned by the local engine for this workspace.
settings.plugins.noInstalled: No installed Codex plugins are enabled for this workspace.
settings.plugins.noMarketplaceMatch: No Codex plugins match the current inventory and search.
settings.plugins.noMcpServers: No MCP servers are currently configured or contributed by enabled plugins.
settings.plugins.panelDescription: Codex marketplace plugins, MCP, and hooks.
settings.plugins.selectConversationInfo: Select a conversation to enable direct MCP tool calls.
settings.plugins.subtitle: Marketplace plugins, installed plugin mentions, hooks, apps, and MCP servers
settings.privacy.dangerousModeAuditDescr: Critical bypass turns are written to the local audit log.
settings.privacy.providerKeyHandlingDesc: Keys are masked in the UI and excluded from exported profiles and Codex config writes.
settings.privacy.subtitle: Secrets and dangerous mode stay audited
settings.profile.subtitle: Export and import workbench profiles without API keys
settings.profile.title: UI profiles
settings.relay.activeModelsHelp: Comma-separated model IDs enabled for this channel
settings.relay.addDescription: Create a relay channel with API format, base URL, models, aliases, and rates.
settings.relay.channelsSubtitle: Manage relay channels, models, rates, activation, and testing from one place.
settings.relay.charityNotice: Charity Station Features: Enabled daily check-in and automated reminders (once per day).
settings.relay.editDescription: Update this channel without exposing the saved API key.
settings.relay.enableTieredContextHelp: Enable tiered pricing based on Prompt Token context ranges (defaulting to 0~250K and 250K~1M)
settings.relay.fetchModelsNeedBaseUrl: Enter Base URL before fetching models
settings.relay.fetchModelsNeedKey: Enter API Key (or save the channel first) before fetching models
settings.relay.fetchModelsSuccessLarge: Fetched {count} models. Use search to find specific ones.
settings.relay.formSubtitle: {title}: configure endpoint, models, aliases, and model rates.
settings.relay.groupRatioHelp: Billing multiplier for this group (e.g. 1.0 = standard, 0.8 = VIP discount)
settings.relay.keyPool: Same-Group Key Rotation Pool (one key per line)
settings.relay.keyPoolHelp: Load-balances multiple keys for this group automatically
settings.relay.manualModelsHint: You can still type model IDs manually, or fetch and select from the list.
settings.relay.memberReadOnly: You can view and activate relay channels. Only admins can create, edit, or delete channels.
settings.relay.modeAdvancedHint: Advanced Mode: Configure multi-group per station, group ratios, key rotation pools, fallbacks, and tiered context multipliers.
settings.relay.modeFastHint: Fast Mode: Simple single API key configuration for standalone relays.
settings.relay.modelRatesAdvancedHelp: Advanced mode unlocks raw USD-per-million token prices plus the four multipliers. Leave multipliers at 1 for normal pricing.
settings.relay.modelRatesDescription: Each active model has input, cache read, cache write, and output multipliers. Defaults are 1.
settings.relay.modelRatesFastHelp: Fast mode hides raw USD price fields. Use the four multipliers only; default 1 means normal pricing.
settings.relay.modelRatesListHelp: Fast mode keeps pricing simple: set per-model multipliers for input, output, cache read, and cache write. Every value defaults to 1.
settings.relay.modelRatesNoModels: Add active models first, then configure their multipliers.
settings.relay.modelRatesTieredHint: Tiered context is enabled. These model multipliers remain visible as base multipliers and are multiplied together with group and tier ratios. Keep them at 1 unless you need a per-model override.
settings.relay.name: Name
settings.relay.noModelsFetched: No models yet. Fill Base URL + API Key, then fetch.
settings.relay.quotaUsd: Total Quota / Max Usage Limit ($ USD)
settings.relay.quotaUsdHelp: Maximum total accumulated usage quota for this relay station in USD. Default is unlimited.
settings.relay.removeSelectedFromActive: Toggle removes models already in the active list
settings.relay.searchPlaceholder: Search relay channels, models, tags, or URLs
settings.relay.startingCodexTest: Opening a new Codex test conversation...
settings.relay.stationTypeHelp: Select station property tag. Default is Third Party. Wealthy tag renders gold coin icon, Charity enables check-in & daily reminders.
settings.relay.testWithCodexHint: Activate this relay, open a new read-only Fast conversation, and send a real turn through Codex app-server.
settings.relay.tieredContextHelp: Tiered pricing multipliers based on Prompt Token context ranges
settings.security.passwordHint: Members can change their own password. An animated captcha is required before the change is applied.
settings.security.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
settings.security.registrationHint: When on, visitors can create member accounts from the login page.
settings.security.subtitle: Registration, captcha, Google Authenticator (TOTP), and admin defaults.
settings.security.title: Security
settings.security.totpHint: Scan the QR with Google Authenticator / Authy. Required at login when enabled.
settings.security.totpSystemOff: System TOTP is disabled by admin.
settings.session.permissionPresetDescrip: Controls sandbox and approval policy for new turns in this UI session.
settings.session.reasoningStrengthDescri: Sends the selected effort as turn/start.effort for this session.
settings.session.selectedModelDescriptio: Session model for the next turn (toolbar). Engine default is managed in Codex Engine Config.
settings.session.subtitle: Per-session overrides used by the next turn
settings.skills.empty: No skills discovered by the local Codex engine.
settings.skills.subtitle: Skill roots, enablement, and local markdown previews
settings.skills.title: Skills
settings.subtitle: Live Codex user config, theme plugins, and resizable workbench preferences
settings.theme.errorImageUrl: Theme image media must be http(s), blob, or data:image URLs.
settings.theme.errorPreviewHex: Primary, secondary, and background must be hex colors.
settings.theme.errorToneHex: Theme tone and scene colors must be hex colors.
settings.theme.errorVideoUrl: Theme video background must be http(s), blob, or data:video URLs.
settings.theme.name: Name
settings.theme.previewDescription: A selected background image will skin the shell, default workbench, and composer surfaces.
settings.title: Settings
settings.usage.rechargeHint: Admin only: add, set, or subtract member credits. Notes are stored in the ledger.
settings.usage.subtitle: Credits, recharges, spend trends, and ledger (Sub2API-style).
settings.usage.title: Usage & Billing
settings.workspace.files.noFileSelected: Select a file to preview or edit.
settings.workspace.subtitle: Browse and edit workspace files from Settings
workspace.description: Every new chat starts at ~/ until you confirm a workspace.
workspace.label: Workspace
workspace.noFolders: No folders loaded for this path.
workspace.pickerSubtitle: Browse folders or type a path directly.
workspace.required: Choose and confirm a workspace for this new conversation before sending.
workspace.sshHelp: Generate a key if needed, copy the public key to the remote host, then run the SSH command once in a terminal to accept the host fingerprint before browsing folders.
workspace.title: Choose workspace for this conversation
